response.justification: While the candidate is a skilled software engineer with proven IDE development experience (LabWindows™/CVI at National Instruments) and strong software craftsmanship practices, the role requires strong expertise in Java and Kotlin with Spring ecosystem knowledge. This resume shows minimal Java experience and no Kotlin background, with recent 10+ years focused on TypeScript/Node.js web and backend development. The IDE experience, though relevant, is over a decade old and would require significant ramp-up on current JVM tooling and enterprise frameworks.
strengths: Demonstrated IDE development expertise from LabWindows™/CVI role at National Instruments
Strong software craftsmanship culture with proven TDD, CI/CD, and architectural thinking across complex systems
Full-stack engineer who has shipped high-performance systems (Dunnhumby microservices, event-driven GIS platform)
concerns: Minimal Java experience and no Kotlin expertise despite these being core job requirements
No demonstrated experience with Spring ecosystem, Hibernate, Quarkus, or enterprise frameworks
IDE tooling experience is dated (2014-2015); recent decade focused on web services and TypeScript, moving away from JVM specialization

key_factors.factor: IDE development background and software engineering fundamentals
